description: Business accounts. Bifolio from a ledger. In a mixture of Judaeo-Arabic and Arabic script. Dating: 11th century. There are several fingerprints. Most entries are boxed and/or crossed out. Many entries are introduced with the phrase "al-bāqī ʿinda X baʿda qaṭʿ al-ḥisāb." People mentioned: ʿAlī; al-ṣabiyy al-ṣaghīr; Abū Isḥāq Ibrāhīm; Maḥbūb; Aḥmad; ghulām al-ṭabīb; Ḥasan; Ismāʿīl; Mūsā; Ibn al-Ashqar; Ḥayyim; Abū l-Ḥasan; Ḥasan b. Naḥum; Saʿāda; Ḥusayn b. L[...]; Ḥayyim b. Madīnī, probably identical with the well-known Abū Zikrī Ḥayyim b. ʿAmmār Ibn al-Madīnī ("son of the Palermitan"). T-S AS 201.82, T-S NS 83.18, and T-S NS 320.125 probably all come from the same ledger. ASE
description_he: רשימה או טבלה בArabic, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
